channel.title: Comments for page "A Farewell To Arms"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Farewell To Arms"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I think I found a continuity error here.

Off-guard, the chemical mutant was sliced in half by Palanez's energy blade

She had just torn one of them in half

So who cut the mutant in half? Palenez or Figueroa?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to know what happened to the plasma T. Rex as it never killed it's still chewing the turret last we heard.

item.title: Re: X Man syndrome at its finest.
item.content:encoded: The O5s decided whether it was a desperate enough situation to change approaches, and the majority ruled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely a bit cheesy, but the well written action makes up for it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! The revolution has won!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Armando gets two thumbs up. I hope that's okay, unlike him I only have the two.

In all seriousness, I like this one a little better than "Shape of a Gun" if only because it feels like there is a little more threat to the protagonists, even if that threat is rather quickly nullified. Monica is great an all, but Armando is slightly more compelling for me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think I'll ever not be cheesy.

It brings me great joy and satisfaction to know people are having as much fun reading this as I had writing it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheesy and predictable at times, but the action is so addictive that I can overlook that simply because of how much fun I had while reading it. Armando rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on cracking +50!

item.title: Re: X Man syndrome at its finest.
item.content:encoded:
It is NOT a superhero site. This, this is madness!!! This goes against the very guidelines that were imposed on us when we first joined the site!!

SCP-353 and SCP-2800 would like a word.

On a more serious note: This is a tale, and part of what makes tales wonderful is that they don't have to abide by the 'rules' of the Foundation — tonally, narratively, or otherwise.

Also, let's not forget: This is a writing community built upon the premise that there's a global conspiracy surrounding the magical poop-smeared murder-statue that breaks your neck if you blink at it.

I think the superhero stuff is fine.

item.title: Re: X Man syndrome at its finest.
item.content:encoded: X Man syndrome is a problem for SCPS, not tales

item.title: Re: X Man syndrome at its finest.
item.content:encoded: Have you not read many tales before? It's very common for them to break from the usual tone of the website. There are plenty out there which are far more silly and outrageous than this one.

item.title: Sorry you didn't like my story :(
item.content:encoded:
clinical, professional and studious containment of anomalous activity

Yep. That's what the O5s are doing. This story is set in a time when those things are starting to break down.

guidelines

Precisely. To quote Captain Barbossa, they're "more what you call guidelines than actual rules."

Why not use a platoon of memetically controlled guards, all armed with the super suits, led by a cybernetically enhanced SCP- 682??

It would have made the story too long.

If you are going to go against the primordial fabric of this site, then why not go all the way???

This, is actually pretty solid advice, and I will take it to heart in future works.

item.title: Re: X Man syndrome at its finest.
item.content:encoded:
Take my
-1

item.title: X Man syndrome at its finest.
item.content:encoded: Seriously guys?? Seriously?? The foundation is a site about the clinical, professional and studious containment of anomalous activity. It is NOT a superhero site. This, this is madness!!! This goes against the very guidelines that were imposed on us when we first joined the site!! Excluding that, does the foundation in this story only have 3 super suits?? Why not use a platoon of memetically controlled guards, all armed with the super suits, led by a cybernetically enhanced SCP- 682?? If you are going to go against the primordial fabric of this site, then why not go all the way???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Old Vegas is definitely not the only mutant community.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Armando and Monica would be a hell of a duo.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So awesome!

I'm definitely interested in seeing the results of the Apotheosis on other anomalies; Armando was a fun addition to this.

I do have a quick question, are these guys associated with Monica and her crew, or are they part of a different group of mutants?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
X-Men vs. super-science, and I'm glad Armando's there. :D This was awesome.

an anchor can hold a boat in place but it can't stop it from sinking.

My thoughts exactly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
because whomst the fuck needs arms anyways
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: O5-2 was never much of fitness guy. Bet he regrets that now. +1

item.title: DOOM
item.content:encoded:
New tale in the Apotheosis scenario setting. Grab some popcorn, the fireworks have begun.

Image is CC-BY-SA 2.0, sourced here.

Huge thanks to my wonderful teammates CadaverCommander, Modern_Erasmus, and fieldstone for encouragement and crit.

Gigantic thanks to the incredibly charming, handsome, and cool Weryllium for allowing me to use his SCP-3589 in this.

 
WORD OF GOD

Figueroa is a Dragon Ball Z fan.
